title: OACP Handoff Payload (v1)
description: Payload schema for messages with payloadType = 'handoff-v1'. Models a governed transfer from one participant/role to another. A handoff is NOT a binding (see binding.schema.json) — bindings are authority assignments; handoffs are governed transitions between participants/roles. A handoff may reference a binding via bindingRef but is a distinct artifact. The handoff carries route context (which manifest-declared route is being followed) and the next participant's payloadType expectation.

handoffId.description: Stable identifier for this handoff definition (e.g., 'classifier-to-extractor'). May repeat across runs.

handoffInstanceId.description: Stable identifier for THIS specific handoff event within a run. Unique per (tenantId, runId).

handoffStatus.description: Reserved handoff status enumeration. State transitions follow REQUESTED → VALIDATING → (ACCEPTED | DENIED). ACCEPTED → COMPLETED | FAILED | EXPIRED | CANCELLED. DENIED, FAILED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED are terminal.

role.description: Source role (e.g., 'CLASSIFIER'). Roles are domain-specific labels declared in the source's capability manifest.

participantId.description: Target participant identity. Per spec §3b allowedHandoffs, this MUST be in the source's manifest's allowedHandoffs[].

route.description: Optional route context — which manifest-declared route this handoff follows.

bindingRef.description: Reference to the source's binding under which the handoff is authorized. recordType MUST be BINDING_RECORD.

targetBindingRef.description: Optional reference to the target's binding (when pre-allocated). recordType MUST be BINDING_RECORD.

payloadType.description: The payload type of the artifact being handed off (e.g., 'classification-result-v1'). MUST match the target's manifest's inputPayloadTypes.

payloadRef.description: Reference to the artifact being handed off (the actual content the target receives).

validationRefs.description: ValidationResult references for gates that have passed prior to handoff (e.g., schema validation, capability validation).

denialReason.description: Required when handoffStatus is DENIED, FAILED, EXPIRED, or CANCELLED. Reserved values: HANDOFF_NOT_DECLARED, TARGET_NOT_AVAILABLE, TARGET_BINDING_INVALID, PAYLOAD_TYPE_MISMATCH, VALIDATION_FAILED, TTL_EXCEEDED, RUN_CANCELLED. Custom values use namespace prefixes per §2b.7.
